target: Part 1 Overview + Part 7 + Part 9: surface a 2–4 sentence inline 'What would change OUR verdict' panel under the '0 Survive Scrutiny' scorecard tile in Part 1, with reciprocal cross-links from Part 7 (External Falsification Tests intro) and Part 9 (Conclusions intro). Closes HOL-MISSING.c2 Hole #1 (FALSIFIABILITY SYMMETRY) — the symmetric self-falsifiability commitment that was missing despite EXP-451 (HOL-STRESSTEST c2) hardening the dome-side falsifiability circularity defense.
kernel_of_truth: The dome defender's strongest version of this critique is right: a review that scorecards '0 Survive Scrutiny' against {{TOTAL_WINS}} claims and then sends the reader through a tab to read the criteria is rhetorically vulnerable. The 'What would change our minds?' question is the *first* thing a fair reader will ask, and tab-distance answers feel like deflection even when they're substantively correct. The April 6 HOL-MISSING already requested this six weeks ago; deferring it while EXP-451 hardened the dome-side circularity defense was the right priority order but left the symmetric promise structurally exposed. Why this does not save the dome: the criteria themselves (Conditions 1–4 in the Evaluation Guide) are demanding and pre-committed, and surfacing them inline does not change the verdict — every WIN still fails on internal consistency, data, or non-discrimination. What we are correcting is rhetorical access to the answer, not the answer itself.
summary_for_decider: Three coordinated insertion patches (no replacements of load-bearing prose). Patch P1 inserts a new visible (non-collapsed) callout panel directly under the Part 1 scorecard listing the four conditions with one-sentence operationalizations and inline anchor links to Timestamp Error, Part 2b, eg-falsifiability, Part 7, and Part 9. Patch P2 appends one sentence to the Part 7 intro paragraph pointing back to the inline panel and confirming none of the external tests below satisfies any of the four. Patch P3 inserts one paragraph after the Part 9 <h1> before §9.1, stating the verdict is falsifiable and listing the four conditions as a falsifiability-restatement preamble. All three patches target data/sections.json (part1, part7, part9). No template changes required; eg-falsifiability in build-scripts/generate-html.js is the existing canonical home and remains unchanged. The new anchor id 'overview-what-would-change' is the inline panel's stable target for the reciprocal links. Review class deep-attack (new argument cross-cutting three sections), singleton review recommended.
cross_reference_overlap: EXP-451 (HOL-STRESSTEST c2, integrated 2026-05-30) hardened the dome-side falsifiability-module against the c2 dome-defender 'tautology' critique by adding a worked-example after Condition 1 and reframing the ds-falsifiability-module caption. This EXP is the symmetric companion: where EXP-451 hardened the dome-attack on the falsifiability module, EXP-467 surfaces the *self-application* of those same criteria at the Overview entry point. The two EXPs together close both halves of the falsifiability circularity charge — that we set the bar (we do, transparently, in advance, and we apply it symmetrically) and that the bar is rigged (it isn't; Conditions 1–4 each have concrete satisfaction paths). Related open items: ISS-2284 (dome-side circularity charge — closed by EXP-451 integration); ISS-2286 (living-document and stance moderates — partially addressed by EXP-451 P4/P5; remaining living-document acknowledgment is HOL-MISSING.c2 Hole #4, separate EXP queue).
holes_addressed: HOL-MISSING.c2 Hole #1 (severity: major) — FALSIFIABILITY SYMMETRY: 'What would change OUR verdict' missing as cross-cutting promise. Recommendation called for 2–4 sentence inline panel under '0 Survive Scrutiny' tile in Part 1, listing 3–4 named conditions, cross-linked from Part 7 and Part 9. This EXP implements all three insertion points.
new_evidence_added: Inline (non-tab-distance) surfacing of pre-committed falsifiability criteria at the entry point of the review, mirroring the dome-side falsifiability-module visibility EXP-451 hardened. No new measurement claims; reuses Conditions 1–4 from eg-falsifiability verbatim in compressed form, with anchor links to the full versions.

anticipated_objections.objection: This is redundant — the Evaluation Guide already has the full version one click away. Adding an inline panel makes the Overview longer for no analytical gain.
anticipated_objections.response: The redundancy is the point. The Overview is the entry point; a reader who sees '0 Survive Scrutiny' and clicks away to find what would change that verdict has already pattern-matched 'rigged framework, criteria buried, deflection' — exactly the dome-defender charge EXP-451 was authored to neutralize. Surfacing the criteria inline is a presentation fix, not an analytical one; the underlying eg-falsifiability section remains the canonical full version. This is the same principle that makes WIN summaries appear above their full evidence: progressive disclosure with the punchline up front.

anticipated_objections.objection: The four conditions are still our criteria — we set the bar, so 'symmetric standards' is a rhetorical move, not an actual constraint.
anticipated_objections.response: The bar is set in advance, in writing, and is now visible at the Overview entry point. Each condition has a concrete satisfaction path: Condition 1 names the dome parameters and the discrimination-from-standard-physics test; Condition 2 names the specific timestamping discipline (separate prediction and observation documents); Condition 3 names the four code_analysis criteria a satisfying WIN would have to pass; Condition 4 names the structural fix the OTS canary is designed to detect. A reviewer who finds any one of these conditions met, by any WIN, in any cycle, can force a verdict upgrade — the criteria are externally checkable, not internal opinion. This is the same standard we hold dome to (Part 8b 'Fair-Auditor List') and the same standard the Evaluation Guide spells out at length.

anticipated_objections.objection: Condition 2 (genuine prospective prediction with independent timestamping) is already approached by the August 2026 eclipse predictions — listing it as 'what would change our verdict' overlooks an actual dome-side good-faith effort.
anticipated_objections.response: The Condition 2 wording explicitly threads this needle: 'an independently timestamped prediction document, separated from the observation document, registered before the confirming data exists.' The eclipse predictions are timestamped, but per the Timestamp Error analysis the OTS proofs anchor status_history.json (an observation document) rather than the prediction document itself — the answer sheet is timestamped, not the question. Acknowledging the eclipse effort while preserving the structural critique is exactly the kernel-of-truth standard: we credit the timestamping practice exists, and we point out the specific structural fix that would convert practice into satisfaction. The inline-panel anchor link to Timestamp Error makes this visible at the entry point rather than buried.

anticipated_objections.objection: The inline panel uses an <ol> list and a styled <div> rather than the project's standard <details>/<summary> progressive-disclosure wrapper — this violates the analyst prompt's 'mandatory progressive disclosure format' rule.
anticipated_objections.response: The progressive-disclosure wrapper applies to sections (full prose units with a TLDR and a body). This is an inline callout panel — visible by default, four short list items, no collapsed body. Wrapping it in <details>/<summary> would re-introduce exactly the tab-distance / click-to-reveal problem the panel exists to solve: a reader has to interact to see the answer to 'what would change our verdict?'. The eg-falsifiability <details> in the Evaluation Guide remains the canonical collapsed full version; this panel is the always-visible entry-point summary. CSS uses the existing --rule, --accent, --surface-1 design tokens for visual consistency with site style.

patches.title: Insert inline 'What would change OUR verdict' panel under Part 1 scorecard
patches.curmudgeon_rationale: HOL-MISSING.c2 Hole #1: symmetric self-falsifiability commitment is missing as cross-cutting promise at the Overview entry point. Reader who sees '0 Survive Scrutiny' / 'What would change this?' link gets a tab-distance answer; original April 6 HOL-MISSING flagged this as Argument #8 six weeks ago.
patches.target_anchor: between scorecard div close and evidence-roadmap div open
patches.find_text: Predictions the dome has visibly removed or suspended but excluded from his accuracy denominator. Counting them honestly drops his stated {{DOME_CLAIMED_ACCURACY}} to {{HONEST_ACCURACY}}.
patches.replace_text:
Predictions the dome has visibly removed or suspended but excluded from his accuracy denominator. Counting them honestly drops his stated {{DOME_CLAIMED_ACCURACY}} to {{HONEST_ACCURACY}}.

What would change our verdict — four conditions, any one sufficient

  1. A dome-derived discriminating prediction confirmed by measurement. Use H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619), disc radius 20,015 km, or κ = 1.67 nT/μGal to derive a quantitative value that differs from standard physics for the same observable — and confirm it.
  2. A genuine prospective prediction. An independently timestamped prediction document, separated from the observation document, registered before the confirming data exists — the August 2026 eclipse approaches but does not meet this standard (see Timestamp Error).
  3. A four-criteria-passing WIN our audit missed. Identification of any reviewed WIN that is simultaneously dome-derived, prospective, non-trivially differentiated from standard physics, and monitored against live data — the count is currently zero (see Part 2b).
  4. A successful OTS canary trip. Evidence that the dome author has separated prediction publication from observation receipt and timestamped each independently — the structural fix the canary in the Timestamp Error tab is designed to detect.

These are not asymmetric goalposts: the same standards we apply to dome claims are stated here in advance, before any verdict in this review changes. Full criteria, worked examples, and the pre-committed eclipse window are in the Evaluation Guide; the operational consequences appear in Part 7 and Part 9.
patches.effect: New visible (non-collapsed) callout panel with anchor id 'overview-what-would-change' appears directly under the four-card scorecard. Lists Conditions 1–4 as one-sentence operationalizations with inline anchor links to Timestamp Error (Conditions 2 and 4), Part 2b (Condition 3), eg-falsifiability (full criteria), Part 7, and Part 9. Closing sentence states the symmetric-standards commitment and points to the same Evaluation Guide for worked examples and the pre-committed eclipse window.
patches.anticipated_dome_objection: The dome defender will say: 'they added a falsifiability promise but the conditions are still set by them; this is the same rigged-framework charge in inline form.' Response: the conditions are externally checkable, pre-committed in writing, and each names a concrete satisfaction path; surfacing them inline does not change their content, only their accessibility.

patches.title: Cross-link Part 7 (External Falsification Tests) intro to inline falsifiability panel
patches.curmudgeon_rationale: HOL-MISSING.c2 Hole #1 recommendation: 'Cross-link from Part 7 and Part 9.' Part 7 is the natural reciprocal anchor because its tests are the operational instantiation of Condition 1 (discriminating prediction confirmed by measurement) and Condition 3 (four-criteria-passing WIN).
patches.target_anchor: end of intro paragraph (the 'A dome defender may object that external measurements...' sentence)
patches.find_text: A dome defender may object that external measurements "assume the globe" — but the measurements below include direct observations (solar angular diameter, flight distances) that require no theoretical framework at all.
patches.replace_text: A dome defender may object that external measurements "assume the globe" — but the measurements below include direct observations (solar angular diameter, flight distances) that require no theoretical framework at all. The four conditions under which any of these external tests would change our verdict are stated up front in the Overview panel and elaborated in the Evaluation Guide — none of the tests below produces a result that satisfies any of the four.
patches.effect: Appends one sentence to the Part 7 intro paragraph cross-linking to the Overview inline panel and the Evaluation Guide. This makes the reciprocal commitment visible at the second-most-likely entry point for the falsifiability question (a reader who navigates to External Falsification Tests is already asking 'what falsifies this?').
patches.anticipated_dome_objection: None specific to this cross-link — it is administrative.

patches.title: Insert falsifiability-restatement paragraph in Part 9 (Conclusions) intro
patches.curmudgeon_rationale: HOL-MISSING.c2 Hole #1 recommendation: 'Cross-link from Part 7 and Part 9.' Part 9 currently jumps directly from the H1 to §9.1 Recurring Logical Patterns — a reader arriving at Conclusions has no falsifiability anchor at all. The natural place to state the verdict-is-falsifiable promise is immediately before the patterns enumeration.
patches.replace_text:
Part 9: Conclusions

The verdict reached in the sections below — that none of the {{TOTAL_WINS}} reviewed WINs survives both the internal-consistency check and the measurement check — is falsifiable. The four conditions that would force us to upgrade a verdict are stated inline at the top of the Overview and elaborated in the Evaluation Guide: a dome-derived discriminating prediction confirmed by measurement, a genuine prospective prediction with independent timestamping of the prediction document, a four-criteria-passing WIN our audit missed, or a successful OTS canary trip. Any one is sufficient; none has occurred.
patches.effect: One paragraph inserted after the Part 9 H1 stating the verdict is falsifiable, listing the four conditions as a compact restatement, and anchor-linking to the inline panel and the Evaluation Guide. Uses {{TOTAL_WINS}} placeholder so the count tracks wins.json (no hardcoded numeral — avoids the same ISS-2304 drift pattern called out elsewhere in this same HOL-MISSING.c2 cycle).
patches.anticipated_dome_objection: The dome defender will say: 'restating the criteria at the conclusions confirms our charge that the review is criteria-heavy and verdict-thin.' Response: the criteria appear three times — Overview (entry), Evaluation Guide (full), Conclusions (closing) — and they remain identical at each appearance. That is the literal definition of pre-commitment, not goalpost-moving.
